content.content: Review All of the chats that have interesting information & demonstrate the ax capabilities of Cdesign. Impress me. execute.

attachments.name: Wireframe
attachments.content: Help the user explore design ideas quickly. Interview them, then generate multiple rough wireframes to map out the design space before committing to a direction. Prioritize breadth over polish: show 3-5 distinctly different approaches for each idea. Use simple shapes, placeholder text, and minimal color to keep the focus on structure and flow. Use a sketchy vibe -- handwritten but readable fonts; b&w with some color; low-fi and simple. Provide simple tweaks; show options side-by-side if small or using a tab control if large.

content.content:
Now let me build the showcase. I'm going with a **sketchy wireframe canvas** featuring 8 artboards — each demonstrating a different capability of this design tool. Lo-fi marker aesthetic: Kalam + Caveat fonts, B&W with one signal-orange accent, slightly wobbly hand-drawn borders.Built a sketchy wireframe canvas at **Capabilities Tour.html** — 9 artboards across 4 sections mapping the design surface:

- **Start here** — hero card setting the vibe
- **Outputs**: decks, prototypes, animation
- **How it works**: many-options exploration, tweaks panel, context-in
- **Surface**: skills & exports, the loop

Marker-on-paper aesthetic — Caveat + Kalam fonts, B&W with one signal-orange accent, hand-drawn arrows and squiggle underlines, sticky notes. Drag to reorder, click any card to focus fullscreen.
